step_by_step_thinking:
To test the statistical significance of the difference in heights among three socioeconomic groups of schoolchildren, the most appropriate statistical test is **One Way Analysis of Variance** (one way ANOVA). Height measurements represent a continuous variable, and since there are more than two groups being compared (three socio-economic groups), ANOVA provides a comprehensive assessment without increasing the risk of Type I error associated with multiple comparisons like repeated t-tests.
\boxed{D} Based on the evidence, the recommended statistical test for assessing differences in heights across three socio-economic groups of schoolchildren is indeed the One Way Analysis of Variance (one way ANOVA).
